description: The act of giving a detailed account of something in words. It is the process of painting a picture with language to create a shared, grounded understanding.
sound_symbolism: The word begins with the thoughtful 'th', requiring the careful observation that good description demands. This flows into the bright 'li', illuminating details like light revealing texture and color. The final 'ma' grounds the description in tangible presence, ensuring words connect to lived reality. This three-part journey—from careful attention through illuminating detail to grounded presence—mirrors the art of description itself: first we observe mindfully, then we illuminate what we see, finally we ground it in shared understanding. The sound moves from breath to brightness to earth.
grammatical_notes: Functions as both verb (to describe) and noun (a description) without morphological change, following the pattern of Mandarin miáoshù (描述), Hindi varnan (वर्णन), and Arabic waṣf (وصف). In analytical languages, the same form naturally expresses both action and result.
